preamble: The shift from a high back rounded nasalized vowel [ũ] to a high front unrounded nasalized vowel [ĩ] involves unrounding and fronting, similar to the oral [u] to [i] or [y] shifts.

examples.note: Fronting and unrounding of high nasalized vowels in certain historical lineages.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
